duas.english: I seek refuge in Allah from the accursed Shaytan
duas.context: Say this immediately when you feel the urge. It is the first shield.

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْفَوَاحِشِ مَا ظَهَرَ مِنْهَا وَمَا بَطَنَ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma inni a'udhu bika min al-fawahish ma zahara minha wa ma batan
duas.english: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from indecency — both what is apparent and what is hidden.
duas.context: A powerful dua specifically for protection from sin and indecency.

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَعِنِّي عَلَى ذِكْرِكَ وَشُكْرِكَ وَحُسْنِ عِبَادَتِكَ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma a'inni ala dhikrika wa shukrika wa husni ibadatika
duas.english: O Allah, help me to remember You, to be grateful to You, and to worship You well.
duas.context: Recite this every morning to begin the day with divine support.

duas.arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ الْعَظِيمَ الَّذِي لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ
duas.transliteration: Astaghfirullaha al-'Azim alladhi la ilaha illa huwa al-Hayyu al-Qayyumu wa atubu ilayh
duas.english: I seek forgiveness from Allah the Almighty, whom there is none worthy of worship except He, the Living, the Sustainer, and I repent to Him.
duas.context: The master dua for repentance (Sayyid al-Istighfar). Say this when you relapse.

duas.arabic: رَبَّنَا ظَلَمْنَا أَنفُسَنَا وَإِن لَّمْ تَغْفِرْ لَنَا وَتَرْحَمْنَا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ
duas.transliteration: Rabbana zalamna anfusana wa in lam taghfir lana wa tarhamna lanakoonanna min al-khasireen
duas.english: Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves. If You do not forgive us and have mercy upon us, we will surely be among the losers.
duas.context: The dua of Prophet Adam and Hawwa — it acknowledges fault and seeks mercy. Use after relapse.

duas.arabic: أَصْبَحْنَا وَأَصْبَحَ الْمُلْكُ لِلَّهِ وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ
duas.transliteration: Asbahna wa asbahal mulku lillah, wal hamdu lillah, la ilaha illallah wahdahu la shareeka lah
duas.english: We have entered the morning, and the sovereignty on this morning belongs to Allah. All praise is for Allah. None has the right to be worshipped except Allah alone.
duas.context: Say this first thing in the morning to start your day with remembrance of Allah.

duas.arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الَّذِي لَا يَضُرُّ مَعَ اسْمِهِ شَيْءٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي السَّمَاءِ وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
duas.transliteration: Bismillahi alladhi la yadurru ma'a ismihi shay'un fil ardi wa la fis sama' wa huwa as-Sami' al-'Alim
duas.english: In the Name of Allah, with Whose name nothing can cause harm on earth or in heaven, and He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
duas.context: Whoever says this 3 times in the morning and evening will not be harmed.

duas.arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي بِنِعْمَتِهِ تَتِمُّ الصَّالِحَاتُ
duas.english: Praise be to Allah by whose grace all good things are completed.
duas.context: Say this when you complete a day clean — it is an act of worship to thank Allah.

duas.english: O Allah, I ask You for patience.
duas.context: Short, powerful, sincere. When the struggle is hard, this dua is enough.
duas.source: Reported by scholars of Islamic supplication

duas.arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنتَ سُبْحَانَكَ إِنِّي كُنتُ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
duas.transliteration: La ilaha illa anta subhanaka inni kuntu min az-zalimin
duas.english: There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers.
duas.context: The dua of Prophet Yunus from the belly of the whale. For moments of feeling trapped.
